todaysEvents.summary: Trump declares Iran hostilities 'terminated' in letter to Congress (May 1): bypasses War Powers 60-day deadline; claims ceasefire 'terminates' hostilities; Republicans defer; Sen. Collins breaks ranks: 'deadline is not a suggestion'; no AUMF passed (PBS, CNBC, CBC, May 1).
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Legal maneuver, not real de-escalation; blockade, vessel seizures, and nuclear stalemate unchanged; Democrats' legal challenge now pending

todaysEvents.summary: Iran submits new proposal via Pakistan: Hormuz reopens if US ends war + lifts blockade; nuclear talks deferred to later phase; Trump 'not satisfied' - 'blast them away' if no deal; Rubio: nuclear must be included; Brent whipsawed $118.03 (Apr 29) to $126 high (Apr 30) to $108.10 (May 1, TradingEconomics) (PBS, Al Jazeera, May 1).
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Phased proposal signals de-escalatory intent but nuclear veto unchanged; Brent pullback on rejection, not Hormuz reopening

todaysEvents.summary: May 2 (Sat): markets closed; ceasefire holds nominally; US blockade intact; Iran holds 3 Western vessels (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas, Euphoria); Trump: 'still in discussions'; Hormuz transits ~13/day; standoff continues; tail 45%.
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: No material change; 'terminated' declaration is diplomatic not operational; structural disruption unresolved

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump's 'terminated' declaration (May 1) and Iran's phased Hormuz proposal create narrow path - Iran accepts nuclear addendum, vessels released, Hormuz reopens under mine-clearing protocol. Narrow (10%) - Trump 'not satisfied' with current proposal; nuclear stalemate persists; mine-clearing ~6 months regardless of deal (PBS); full commercial reopening delayed even on deal. Brent retreats toward $92-98/bbl on deal; iTraxx tightens toward 175bp.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Trump 'terminated' declaration bypasses War Powers legally; nuclear stalemate persists; US blockade and IRGC Hormuz enforcement co-exist; selective bilateral transit; periodic vessel seizures. Iran's phased proposal stalls on nuclear precondition. Brent stabilises $100-115/bbl; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 195-215bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (45%).

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Democrats' legal challenge to 'terminated' declaration succeeds or Iran interprets Trump's 'blast them away' threats as prelude to escalation; IRGC ROE hardens on nuclear impasse; 'shoot and kill' order triggers incident with IRGC small boat. Gulf-wide blockade activated; Kharg Island seizure or Aramco targeting brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; Malaysia June gap becomes July-August crisis. Brent whipsawed to $126 wartime high Apr 30 before pulling back on 'terminated' declaration. Tail 45%.
